chat.author: Joe Hildebrand
chat.text: I can't log in to meetecho, presumably because i didn't register for the meeting.  Therefore, I've stayed up until 330am for nothing.

chat.author: Paul Hoffman
chat.text: But yes, that would be the reason.

chat.author: Joe Hildebrand
chat.text: I guess you don't need my input then.  Good night.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: Only now noticed the problem Joe had.  That is an unfortunate consequence of the way IETF in-person meetings are run.  There are ways to handle this situation, which do require a little bit of preparation, and I feed bad that we didn't address this.

chat.author: Carsten Bormann
chat.text: So, yes, Joe, we do need your input.

chat.author: Martine Lenders
chat.text: +1 for the procedure btw. My wishlist for things that should _not_ be removed so I can continue to work on -dns-cbor are: a way forward towards the e'' extension (https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-ietf-cbor-edn-e-ref/ or something comparable) and extensibility in general that a human readable CDN of DNS records could be provided later on.

chat.author: Martine Lenders
chat.text: And yes also +1 for correct subject line. Makes it much easier to follow the conversation.
